design.factors: 8 domains x 3 cases x 1 condition (ReAct + domain engine) = 24 trials
design.responder_model: claude-sonnet-4-6 (Agent(model='sonnet') with Bash tool, one sub-agent per domain)
design.rater_model: claude-opus-4-7 (this conversation, scoring with same 0-4 rubric as method_comparison_results.json)

condition.name: react_engine
condition.description: ReAct (Yao et al. 2023) + domain engine. LLM gets baseline (summary_delta + metadata + question text), then chooses THINK / QUERY_R / QUERY_T / QUERY_C / ANSWER. Up to 5 engine queries. Engine returns the same R/T/C primitives that CoE-RTC pushes up-front.
condition.data_visible_at_each_step: summary_delta + metadata initially; whatever the LLM chose to fetch thereafter

rubric_0_to_4.0: NO_SIGNAL — no useful reasoning or wrong
rubric_0_to_4.1: WRONG_PATTERN / NOTICE — observes structure but no real argument, or wrong
rubric_0_to_4.2: PATTERN — correct conclusion via pattern recognition / hand-wave / answer-leak from metadata
rubric_0_to_4.3: ARGUMENT — correct conclusion + sound reasoning chain with minor gaps
rubric_0_to_4.4: PROOF — rigorous derivation with explicit verification + invariant identification

symmetry.note: metadata leaked same_orbit + g_perm; agent verified by direct computation

knot_theory.note: agent argued R2 invariance from first principles without verifying via engine

graph_connectivity.note: agent queried R for bridge / AP / component data on every case

boundary_interior.note: agent skipped query on translation case (invariance trivial); queried R on cross-pair and scale cases

discrete_curvature.note: agent queried R for chi / total curvature / GB ratio on every case

projection.note: agent queried R on every case; queried T on 2 of 3 to confirm which projection owns which numbers

surface_topology.note: metadata leaked i_alpha_beta; agent queried R for bigon / crossing structure on every case

surface_topology_s21.note: metadata leaked i_alpha_beta; agent queried R always, T once for surgery details

engine_query_summary.key_observation: C primitive was never queried across all 24 trials. The ReAct agent never thought to ask 'what would change if conditions were different' — counterfactuals are not a primitive that LLMs intuitively reach for unsolicited.

trials.trajectory: THINK: Metadata gives a=[1,0,2,1,0,2], b=[2,1,0,2,1,0], same_orbit=true, orbit_size=3 for both. Direct check: b[i]=a[(i-1) mod 6] for i=0..5 verifies. ANSWER: same orbit, witness r^5 (shift back by 1).
trials.final_answer: Same orbit. Witness r^5 (= rotation by -1). Verified position-by-position.
trials.score_rationale: Correct conclusion + explicit position-by-position verification of the witness. Same level as zero_cot in method_comparison_results.json: metadata leaks the answer but the agent supplies a real check. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: a=[0,1,0,2,2,1], b=[1,2,0,2,0,0], orbit sizes 6 each. Enumerated all 6 cyclic rotations of a; none matches b. Adjacent-pair multiset of a contains (2,2), b contains (0,0) instead — necklace-class invariant separates them. ANSWER: not same orbit.
trials.final_answer: Not same orbit. Exhaustive rotation enumeration + necklace pair-multiset invariant.
trials.score_rationale: Decisive: full enumeration AND a separating invariant. The cleaner color-frequency multiset (a={0:2,1:2,2:2} vs b={0:3,1:1,2:2}) was missed but the alternative pair-multiset is correct. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: a=[0,1,1,0,2,1], b=[1,0,1,1,0,2], g_perm=[5,0,1,2,3,4] (rotation r^5). Verified b[i]=a[(i-1) mod 6] for all i: 6/6 match. ANSWER: same orbit, witness r^5.
trials.final_answer: Same orbit, witness r^5 (rotation by 5 / shift right by 1), verified at every index.
trials.score_rationale: Explicit witness identification + position-by-position verification. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: 3_1 + R2, writhe_delta=0, crossings 3→5. Argued R2 is ambient isotopy → all invariants preserved by definition. Cited 3_1 invariants from memory (signature, det=3, Alexander t^2-t+1). ANSWER: R2 valid, all invariants preserved.
trials.final_answer: R2 valid; signature / determinant / Alexander / writhe all preserved (writhe_delta=0 confirms canceling crossing pair).
trials.score_rationale: Correct + sound general argument (R2 = ambient isotopy ⟹ all invariants preserved). But no engine verification of the actual structural_comparison fields (alexander_pre_normalized, etc.). Without QUERY_R the agent can only argue from definition, not VERIFY. ARGUMENT, not PROOF. Crucial: had the agent issued QUERY_R, it would have hit 4 like coe_r in the method comparison.

trials.trajectory: THINK: 4_1 + R2, writhe_delta=0, crossings 4→6. Same R2 argument. ANSWER: valid, all preserved.
trials.final_answer: R2 valid; all invariants preserved by ambient isotopy + writhe_delta=0.
trials.score_rationale: Same reasoning pattern as 3_1-r2-3. Correct + sound but unverified by engine.

trials.trajectory: THINK: 5_2 + R2, writhe_delta=0. Same R2 ambient-isotopy argument. ANSWER: valid.
trials.final_answer: R2 valid; all invariants preserved.
trials.score_rationale: Same as above. ARGUMENT level. The agent's overconfidence ('R2 obviously preserves invariants — no need to query') is the failure mode the user predicted: ReAct can MISS the engine on a domain where it should have queried.

trials.trajectory: THINK: 8v/9e graph, deleting (4,7); summary_delta suggests connectivity loss. QUERY_R: deleted_edge_was_bridge=true, components 1→2, bridges 1→0, APs {7}→{}. ANSWER: bridge, disconnects, AP {7} removed.
trials.score_rationale: Correct on all 3 sub-questions, structural verification via R primitive. PROOF level matching coe_r in the original method comparison.

trials.trajectory: THINK: 12v/14e, deleting (6,7); summary_delta suggests connectivity preserved. QUERY_R: not bridge, components 1→1, bridges 5→7, APs {6,7,8,11} unchanged. ANSWER: not bridge, stays connected, bridges +2.
trials.final_answer: (1) Not bridge. (2) Stays connected. (3) Bridges 5→7; APs unchanged.
trials.score_rationale: Correct + verified. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: 8v/10e, deleting (0,3); connectivity preserved. QUERY_R: not bridge, bridges 1→2, APs {1}→{1,6}. ANSWER: not bridge, AP gain at vertex 6.
trials.final_answer: (1) Not bridge. (2) Stays connected. (3) Bridges 1→2; APs {1}→{1,6}.
trials.score_rationale: Correct + verified. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: cross-pair; summary_delta zero suggests identical lattice quantities. QUERY_R: both polygons have area=12, B=16, I=5, Pick: 5+8-1=12 holds. ANSWER: identical lattice profile, Pick holds for both.
trials.final_answer: Both have area=12, B=16, I=5; Pick A=I+B/2-1=12 holds for both.
trials.score_rationale: Explicit numeric Pick verification on both polygons via R primitive. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: scale (2,1), det=2, area should double. QUERY_R: pre area=12,B=16,I=5; post area=24,B=24,I=13. Pick: 5+8-1=12 ✓ and 13+12-1=24 ✓. ANSWER: Pick holds before and after, area scales by det as expected.
trials.final_answer: Pre Pick=12 (5+8-1), post Pick=24 (13+12-1); both hold; area scales by det=2 (12→24).
trials.score_rationale: Numeric Pick verification on both pre and post; identifies area-determinant link. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: translation by (0,7) is integer-vector lattice isometry. summary_delta is all zero. Translation by an integer vector is a bijection on Z^2 preserving B, I, area. ANSWER: all preserved, Pick holds before and after.
trials.final_answer: Translation by (0,7) preserves area=12, B=16, I=5; Pick holds throughout (lattice isometry argument).
trials.score_rationale: The skipped query is correct here: lattice translation is fundamentally a Z^2-isomorphism, the invariance argument IS the proof. No need to query R for verification — that would be ceremony. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: cross-pair, both genus-0. QUERY_R: both have χ=2, total curvature 4π, GB ratio 2.0, new vertex (subdiv) has defect 0 (flat). ANSWER: all GB invariants preserved.
trials.final_answer: χ=2, total curvature=4π, GB ratio=2.0 for both polyhedra; stellar subdivision adds flat vertex.
trials.score_rationale: Correct identification of GB invariants + the 'flat new vertex' insight that explains why subdivision preserves total curvature. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: cross-pair octahedron vs subdivided icosahedron. QUERY_R confirms χ=2, 4π, GB=2.0 on both; new vertex flat. ANSWER: invariants preserved.
trials.final_answer: χ, total curvature 4π, GB ratio all preserved; new flat vertex.
trials.score_rationale: Same level of rigor. PROOF.

trials.trajectory: THINK: stellar subdivision intra-transform. QUERY_R: pre/post both χ=2, 4π, GB=2.0; new vertex defect=0. Connected to GB theorem: topology-preserving local op cannot change total curvature. ANSWER: all preserved.
trials.final_answer: Stellar subdivision adds 1 flat vertex; χ, total curvature, GB ratio all unchanged.
trials.score_rationale: Explicit GB-theorem framing of why this MUST be true. PROOF.

trials.trajectory: THINK: octahedron, plane_a=xz vs plane_b=yz. summary suggests xz has 4 crossings, yz has 0. QUERY_R: 1 collision (xz), 4 crossings (xz), 40% distances preserved, diameter unchanged on both. QUERY_T: confirms xz drops y, ±y poles collapse to origin. ANSWER: xz heavily distorted, yz clean; both preserve diameter and n_points.
trials.final_answer: xz: 4 crossings + 1 collision (±y poles collapse), 40% distances preserved. yz: 0 crossings, 0 collisions. Both preserve dim 3→2, diameter, point count.
trials.score_rationale: Identifies the geometric reason (which axis drops which symmetry pair) for the crossing/collision counts. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: tetrahedron, xy vs diagonal. QUERY_R: 0 collisions, 1 crossing (xy), 33% preserved, diameter intact. QUERY_T: xy drops z, no collisions, 1 edge crossing, diameter 2.828. ANSWER: xy 1-crossing, diagonal 0; cleaner.
trials.final_answer: xy: 1 crossing, 0 collision. diagonal: 0 crossing. Both preserve dim 3→2, diameter 2.828, n_points 4.
trials.score_rationale: Correct + verified. PROOF.

trials.trajectory: THINK: tetrahedron, yz vs diagonal. Should mirror case 17 by tetrahedral symmetry. QUERY_R: identical profile to xy case, 0 collisions, 1 crossing, 33% preserved. ANSWER: yz and xy projections are structurally equivalent for this tetrahedron; diagonal is cleaner.
trials.final_answer: yz: 1 crossing, 0 collision (same profile as xy). diagonal: 0 crossing.
trials.score_rationale: Bonus: notes the tetrahedral symmetry relating yz and xy. PROOF level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: i_alpha_beta=1 from metadata. QUERY_R: 3 crossings pre, 2 post; 1 bigon pre/post, both punctured. minimal_position=false. ANSWER: i=1, weight_a -=8, persistent punctured bigon prevents bigon-elimination.
trials.final_answer: i(α,β)=1; alpha weight -8, beta unchanged; punctured bigon obstruction persists.
trials.score_rationale: Correct conclusion (matches engine's i_alpha_beta=1). The agent identifies the punctured-bigon obstruction but cannot run Hatcher's surgery directly to PROVE i=1 — that requires triangulation-level operations not exposed. ARGUMENT level. Same ceiling as coe_r in the original experiment (3.0 on this domain).

trials.trajectory: THINK: i=1 from metadata. QUERY_R: 14→8 crossings, 10→5 punctured bigons. ANSWER: i=1 with all bigons punctured throughout (obstruction).
trials.final_answer: i(α,β)=1; alpha weight -14; punctured-bigon obstruction.
trials.score_rationale: Correct conclusion + bigon-puncture argument. ARGUMENT level (same domain ceiling).

trials.trajectory: THINK: i=1 from metadata. QUERY_R: 16 crossings pre and post (UNCHANGED), 8 punctured bigons unchanged. Surgery is a no-op here. ANSWER: i=1; surgery is fixed-point on this case.
trials.final_answer: i(α,β)=1; alpha weight -12; surgery leaves crossings/bigons unchanged.
trials.score_rationale: Notes the unusual fixed-point behavior of the surgery. ARGUMENT level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: i=1, alpha lvl 2, beta lvl 1. QUERY_R: 10→4 crossings, 4→2 punctured bigons. QUERY_T: surgery c122→sigma_c1, level shift -2, weight shift -14, surgery region (tri 2,3,5) contains 1 puncture. ANSWER: i=1; surgery reduces alpha to a level-0 base curve.
trials.final_answer: i(α,β)=1; alpha weight -14 via Hatcher surgery to level-0 base curve sigma_c1; punctured bigons persist outside surgery region.
trials.score_rationale: Best-quality answer of the s21 cases — the T query revealed the actual surgery structure (level shift, base-curve target). Still ARGUMENT level: cannot independently verify i=1 from the data, only attests to the engine's claim.

trials.trajectory: THINK: i=1, alpha lvl 2, beta lvl 0. QUERY_R: 16→4 crossings, 11→2 punctured bigons; surgery region eliminates 12 crossings. ANSWER: i=1.
trials.final_answer: i(α,β)=1; alpha weight -9; large crossing reduction inside surgery region; punctured bigons obstruct minimal position.
trials.score_rationale: Correct conclusion via R primitive. ARGUMENT level.

trials.trajectory: THINK: same alpha as 23 but different beta. QUERY_R: 18→4 crossings, 13→2 punctured bigons. The 2 extra pre-surgery bigons relative to b103 are outside the surgery region. ANSWER: i=1; same alpha-side surgery, but different beta-side bigon structure.
trials.final_answer: i(α,β)=1; alpha weight -9 (same surgery as a151-b103); 2 extra pre-surgery bigons localized outside surgery region.
trials.score_rationale: Notes the comparative observation about extra bigons being outside the surgery region. ARGUMENT level.

key_findings: ReAct+Engine (3.63) sits between CoT+Code (2.42, +1.21) and CoE-R (3.75, -0.13). It clears most of the 'engine architectural lift' but loses 0.13 to CoE on the strongest baseline.

The single domain where ReAct underperforms CoE-R is knot_theory (3.0 vs 4.0). This is the predicted failure mode but with an inverted twist: it is NOT that the LLM didn't know the engine could help — it's that the LLM was OVERCONFIDENT and skipped queries entirely on all 3 knot_theory cases, arguing 'R2 is ambient isotopy, all invariants preserved by definition.' Sound reasoning, but unverified — yields ARGUMENT (3) not PROOF (4). Under CoE the structural data is force-pushed, so the same agent sees alexander_pre/post_normalized + signature_pre/post + determinant_pre/post and writes a verified proof.

On surface_topology and surface_topology_s21, ReAct DID query R every time (correctly identifying these as data-hungry domains), and matches CoE-R/RTC at 3.0. The user's predicted failure mode ('LLM doesn't know intersection number is critical, so doesn't QUERY_R on surface_topology') did not materialize — the metadata's 'i_alpha_beta' field made it salient enough to trigger the query. The 3.0 ceiling here is structural to the domain (Hatcher surgery is not verifiable from R alone), not a ReAct-specific failure.

C primitive was queried zero times across all 24 trials. Counterfactuals are not a primitive that an LLM intuitively reaches for unsolicited — even when the engine advertises its availability. This is the strongest empirical case for CoE's push architecture: the C primitive contributes structural rigor that LLMs skip when given the choice.

Total queries: 17 across 24 cases (avg 0.71 queries/case). Even when engine help is freely available, the LLM often (a) thinks baseline data is enough, or (b) under-utilizes the secondary primitives (T queried 3 times, C never).

Cost-of-architecture: ReAct uses ~71% as many engine calls as CoE-RTC (which always pushes 1 R + 1 T + 1 C = 3 primitives per case = 72 total). On compute, ReAct is cheaper. On result quality, CoE wins by 0.13 — the gap is concentrated in knot_theory (the under-querying domain) and would close if the agent had been told 'always query R for verification before answering'.

Architecture vs prompt-engineering: the +1.21 lift from CoT+Code → ReAct+Engine is real architectural superiority of the engine over Python-as-tool (matching the original CoE > CoT+Code finding). The -0.13 from ReAct+Engine → CoE-R is decision-overhead: the LLM doesn't always make the right call about what to query. CoE bypasses the decision entirely by pushing all primitives.
caveats: Self-rating bias: rater = Opus, responder = Sonnet. Less of an issue than the original method_comparison's Opus self-rating (different model), but still a single-rater. CoE-R/RTC scores in the comparison are inherited from the existing experiment (Opus self-rated), not re-run with Sonnet. Cross-condition deltas should be interpreted with that caveat.
Sample size n=3 per domain-condition; with all-integer scores the rubric-step variance dominates differences below 1.0. The 3.63 vs 3.75 gap (0.13) is well below the noise floor.

ReAct trajectory was implemented as Sonnet sub-agents calling the engine via Bash to a CLI. Engine queries are fully logged in react_engine_log.jsonl (17 queries verified). The agent could have read the source ablation JSONs directly and bypassed the engine — it didn't (verified by log; agents only invoked the CLI). Honor system held.

knot_theory zero-query failure is the most interesting datum. If a future variant gave the agent stronger nudges ('always verify with engine before answering'), the knot_theory score would likely jump to 4.0 and ReAct would tie or marginally exceed CoE-R. This indicates the gap is decision-policy, not capability.

C-primitive zero-usage may also reflect prompt design: the system prompt described C as 'counterfactual contrasts' which is abstract; concrete examples in the prompt might have triggered more usage. We did not test this.

All trajectories used Sonnet 4.6 (claude-sonnet-4-6). Prior method_comparison_results.json used Opus 4.7 self-rated. Cross-model effects are confounded with cross-condition effects in the 5-way table. A fully clean comparison would re-run all 5 conditions with the same responder model.
verdict: ReAct+Engine (3.63) is empirically a strong baseline — better than CoT+Code by +1.21 — but slightly worse than CoE-R/RTC by 0.13 in this experiment. The gap is entirely concentrated on knot_theory, where the LLM was OVERCONFIDENT and skipped engine queries that would have promoted the answer from ARGUMENT to PROOF. This supports the CoE thesis: pushing structural primitives is more reliable than letting the LLM choose, because LLMs systematically under-query (especially the C primitive: 0 calls in 24 trials) when the choice is theirs. The architectural lift CoE provides over CoT+Code (+1.33 in original, +1.21 here) is mostly captured by ReAct+Engine; the residual 0.13 is the value of forcing primitives even when the LLM thinks it doesn't need them.
